text: A variable \(x\) in \(\forall x P(x) \land Q(x)\) is _____.

a: If any \(x\) satisfies \(P\), then some \(x\) satisfies \(Q\).

text: Which expression correctly means "Some \(x\) is \(P\) and \(Q\)"?
For the domain of employees, if all new employees met the deadline, which is true?
Why is \(\exists x (P(x) \land Q(x))\) true when \(P(x)\) is true for some \(x\)?
answer: Because there is at least one \(x\) where both \(P\) and \(Q\) are true.
b: Because there is at least one \(x\) where both \(P\) and \(Q\) are true.

text: Which variable is bound in \(\forall x P(x)\)?

answer: There is a specific \(x\) with \(P(x)\) and not \(Q(x)\)
b: There is a specific \(x\) with \(P(x)\) and not \(Q(x)\)

text: The scope of a quantifier determines:
answer: Where the quantifier applies in the expression
b: Where the quantifier applies in the expression
d: The truth value of the entire statement

answer: The first requires one \(x\) to satisfy both; the second allows separate \(x\)
